title: RREQ/RREP hop_limit and hop_count not validated before serialization
description:
File: python/src/lichen/loadng/messages.py
Lines: 64, 100

The `hop_limit` and `hop_count` fields are serialized with `& 0xFF` masking but no validation that they are in valid range (0-255 or more strictly 0-15 per MAX_HOP_LIMIT).

Problem: If hop_limit is -1, `(-1) & 0xFF = 255` which silently produces an invalid wire format. If hop_limit is 300, it becomes 44 silently.

Unlike seq_num (which raises LoadngError if out of range), these fields are silently truncated.

Fix: Add validation before serialization:
```python
if not 0 <= self.hop_limit <= MAX_HOP_LIMIT:
    raise LoadngError(f'hop_limit out of range: {self.hop_limit}')
```
close_reason: Added range validation for hop_limit/hop_count in RREQ/RREP.to_bytes (0 to MAX_HOP_LIMIT). Prevents silent truncation of invalid values. Matches seq_num pattern. Verified in messages.py and tests. Commit ffcd355ab.
